questions.explanation: Turning is performed on a lathe, spinning the part to cut cylindrical shapes. Milling is typically used for flat or squared objects.

questions.question: A thread callout reads 'UNC 1/4-20'. What does the '20' represent?
options: The length of the screw in millimeters
questions.explanation: In UNC 1/4-20, 1/4 is the diameter in inches, and 20 signifies the number of threads per inch.

questions.question: What role does Six Sigma and strict quality control play in assembly and fabrication?
options: Improves quality, reduces execution time, and lowers costs
Slows down the assembly line permanently
questions.explanation: Quality control, including Six Sigma methodologies, standardizes manufacturing which improves reputation, reduces waste, and ultimately lowers costs.

questions.question: How do ferrous materials generally differ from non-ferrous materials?
options: Ferrous materials have high weight, are magnetic, and are easily corrodible
Ferrous materials have higher conductivity than non-ferrous
questions.explanation: Ferrous materials (containing iron) are typically heavier, magnetic, and prone to rust (corrosive), whereas non-ferrous (like copper) are non-magnetic and highly conductive.

questions.question: When expressing data, what is the primary difference between a Bar Graph and a Histogram?
options: Bar graphs show percentages; Histograms show absolute numbers
Bar graphs are for qualitative data; Histograms are for quantitative (numerical) data
Histograms use lines; Bar graphs use bars
questions.explanation: Bar graphs typically represent categorical or qualitative data. Histograms represent continuous quantitative or numerical data grouped in bins.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the three ways heat is transferred?
questions.explanation: Heat transfers via Convection (currents in fluids), Radiation (electromagnetic waves through space/vacuum), and Conduction (direct physical contact).

questions.question: What is the Fahrenheit equivalent of 35 degrees Celsius?
What defines a British Thermal Unit (BTU)?
options: Heat to raise 1 gallon of water by 1 degree C
Heat to raise 1 pound of water by 1 degree F
Heat generated by 1 watt in 1 hour
questions.explanation: A BTU is the standard measurement of thermal energy required to raise the temperature of exactly one pound of water by one degree Fahrenheit.

questions.question: When putting out a fire involving oily rags and flammable liquids, which class of fire extinguisher must be used?
questions.explanation: Class B fire extinguishers are specifically for flammable liquids, oils, and greases (like oily rags).

questions.question: In the workplace, Ergonomics is best described as:
options: Fitting the job and workstation to the person
questions.explanation: Ergonomics is the science of designing the workplace, tools, and tasks to fit the physiological capabilities of the worker, preventing strain injuries.

questions.question: Often considered the earliest historical example of engineering, what machine was used to pump water in ancient times?
questions.explanation: The Archimedes screw, invented by Archimedes in the 3rd century BC, is one of the earliest engineering inventions used to elevate water.

questions.question: A 90 lb container rests on a surface with a coefficient of friction of 0.61. How much force is needed to move it?
questions.explanation: Friction Force = Normal Force (Weight) × Coefficient of Friction. 90 lbs × 0.61 = 54.9 lbs.

questions.question: What is the cubic area (volume) of an object that is 5 feet long, 2.5 feet wide, and 3.5 feet tall?
questions.explanation: Volume = Length × Width × Height. 5 × 2.5 × 3.5 = 43.75 cubic feet.

questions.question: Which field combines physical and life sciences with math to convert raw materials into valuable forms?
questions.explanation: Chemical engineering applies chemistry, biology, physics, and math to create, convert, and transport materials efficiently.

questions.question: What type of education and pathway is strictly required to become a licensed Professional Engineer (PE)?
options: A high school diploma and 1 year of work
An Associate's degree and an OSHA certificate
A Bachelor's degree (ABET), passing the FE (EIT) exam, 4 years of practice, and passing the PE exam
questions.explanation: Becoming a licensed engineer requires an ABET accredited Bachelor's degree, passing the Fundamentals of Engineering (FE) exam, practicing for 4 years, and passing the final PE exam.

questions.question: In order to reduce friction and heat between a workpiece and a cutting tool on a lathe or mill, what is commonly used?
questions.explanation: Cutting fluid (coolant) lubricates the cut, drastically reducing friction and carrying away intense heat from the tool interface.

questions.question: The ability of a material to be stretched into a long, thinner shape without breaking is called:
questions.explanation: Ductility is the property allowing deformation under tensile stress (stretching into a wire). Malleability is deformation under compressive stress (hammering into a sheet).

questions.question: When working with heavy machine tools, which of the following is ALWAYS required?
options: Removing the safety guards for better visibility
questions.explanation: PPE (Safety glasses, safety shoes, tight clothing/no jewelry) is always mandatory when operating tools and machines.

questions.question: Which process is universally used for mass production of plastic parts by heating plastic, injecting it, and extracting parts from a mold?
questions.explanation: Injection molding is the standard process for mass-producing complex plastic components rapidly and uniformly.

questions.question: To properly use a drill press to drill a hole near one end of a ruler or thin workpiece, you must:
options: Hold it tightly with a leather glove
Securely clamp the workpiece in a vise
questions.explanation: Thin objects like rulers can easily catch the drill bit, spinning violently ('helicoptering'). They must ALWAYS be securely clamped to the table or in a vise.

questions.question: Which of the following materials is most appropriate for a highly corrosion resistant valve?
questions.explanation: Brass (a non-ferrous alloy of copper and zinc) is highly resistant to corrosion, making it a standard choice for valves and plumbing.

questions.question: Person A weighs 100 lbs and Person B weighs 150 lbs on a 15' seesaw. If Person A sits 9 ft from the fulcrum, how far should Person B sit to balance?
questions.explanation: Lever balance equation: W1 × D1 = W2 × D2.  100 × 9 = 900.  900 = 150 × D2.  D2 = 900 / 150 = 6 feet.

questions.question: Calculate the flow out of a pipe per hour if the flow rate is 180 gallons per minute.
questions.explanation: 180 gallons per minute × 60 minutes per hour = 10,800 gallons/hour.

questions.question: What is the primary role of a 'Test Engineer'?
options: To verify whether a prototype meets design, quality, and performance objectives
To sell the product to consumers
questions.explanation: Test Engineers focus on verifying functionality, ensuring the prototype adheres to safety standards and meets performance goals before mass production.

questions.question: How much does one standard US gallon of water weigh?
questions.explanation: One gallon of water weighs approximately 8.34 pounds at room temperature.

questions.question: Which of the following designates a ferrous metal?
questions.explanation: Ferrous metals contain iron. Mild steel is a common iron alloy.

questions.question: The resistance a metal has to being pulled apart is known as its:
questions.explanation: Tensile strength measures the force required to pull something such as rope, wire, or a structural beam to the point where it breaks.

questions.question: Which of the following represents a nondestructive test?
options: Hardness indentation testing that mars the surface
questions.explanation: Nondestructive testing (NDT), such as vibration analysis or ultrasounds, analyzes the properties of a material without causing damage.

questions.question: Steel reinforcing bars (rebar) are added to concrete primarily to resist the force of:
questions.explanation: Concrete has excellent compressive strength but very poor tensile strength. Rebar is added to handle tensile (pulling/stretching) loads.

questions.question: Gauge pressure at sea level is exactly:
questions.explanation: Gauge pressure uses atmospheric pressure as its zero point. Therefore, standard atmospheric pressure at sea level reads as 0 psi on a gauge.

questions.question: A solenoid works on the principal of:
questions.explanation: A solenoid uses an electrical coil wound around a core to generate an electromagnetic force that mechanically moves an armature.

questions.question: Pneumatic directional control valves are sometimes sized just for pipe convenience. However, a more efficient method is to size them according to:
options: The physical color of the valve
The length of the electrical wire
questions.explanation: The flow coefficient (Cv) accurately specifies the liquid/gas flow capacity of a valve, ensuring the system isn't choked by undersizing.

questions.question: A photo detector converts light directly into:
questions.explanation: Photoelectric sensors detect the presence of light and translate that physical phenomena into an electrical analog or digital signal.

questions.question: If contaminants begin to block a fluid flow system, the first place the blockage typically negatively impacts is:
options: The suction side of the system's pump
questions.explanation: Filters and strainers are typically located on the pump's inlet. Contamination clogging here causes pump cavitation on the suction side.

questions.question: The speed of a pneumatic cylinder is affected mainly by:
options: The compressibility of air and flow rate
The color of the cylinder housing
The weight of the system reservoir
The type of oil in the neighboring hydraulic system
questions.explanation: Unlike rigid hydraulics, pneumatic speed is heavily dependent on the compressibility of the gas and the flow valves restricting air entering/exiting.

questions.question: In a manufacturing facility, undercutting is a condition that generally occurs when:
options: A saw blade isn't sharp enough
The welding current is too high
The plastic injection mold is cold
questions.explanation: Undercutting is a dangerous weld defect where too high of a welding current melts a groove into the base metal, weakening the cross-section.

questions.question: When milling aluminum on a standard vertical mill, the RPM speed should generally be set:
questions.explanation: Aluminum is a soft metal; machining it optimally requires high spindle speeds (RPM) and fast feed rates to prevent material from galling.

questions.question: When a force of 10 pounds is applied to the West and a perpendicular force of 25 pounds is applied to the South, what is the approximate resultant force?
questions.explanation: Using the Pythagorean theorem (A^2 + B^2 = C^2) for vectors: 10^2 + 25^2 = C^2. 100 + 625 = 725. The square root of 725 is approximately 26.9 (27 pounds).

questions.question: What is considered a positive example of corrective action in a manufacturing setting?
options: A change in the maintenance procedure to prevent future failure
Ignoring the error if it only happens once
questions.explanation: Corrective action eliminates the root cause of an identified nonconformity to prevent recurrence, such as rewriting a flawed maintenance procedure.

questions.question: When storing compressed gas cylinders, it is paramount that they be chained to a rigid object and:
options: Stored lying flat on the floor
Painted bright red regardless of gas type
questions.explanation: Cylinders must be chained upright to prevent falling and decapitating the valve, and stored in ventilated areas to prevent gas buildup if a leak occurs.

questions.question: When lead-acid batteries are charging, they require ventilation because they give off:
questions.explanation: The charging process of a lead-acid battery electrolyzes the water in the electrolyte, releasing a highly explosive mixture of hydrogen and oxygen.

questions.question: When measuring a hole with a micrometer, the reading is 2.468 inches for a hole that is specified to be bored to 2.473 inches. The hole is considered to be:
questions.explanation: 2.473 (specified) - 2.468 (actual) = 0.005. Because the actual hole is smaller than the target, it is 0.005 inches undersize.

questions.question: The junction of two dissimilar metals that produces a voltage proportional to the amount of heat applied is a:
questions.explanation: A thermocouple utilizes the Seebeck effect, creating a millivolt signal between two dissimilar metals when subjected to a temperature gradient.

questions.question: A commercial freezer has an internal temperature of 22 degrees F. The outside room is 78 degrees F. In which direction will the heat flow?
options: Heat will flow out of the freezer to the room
Heat will flow into the freezer from the surrounding room
Cold will flow out of the freezer
questions.explanation: According to the laws of thermodynamics, heat always flows from hotter regions to colder regions. Cold does not 'flow', heat simply evacuates.

questions.question: Which basic electrical equation demonstrates Ohm's law?
questions.explanation: Normally V = I x R. Rearranged for Resistance: R = V/I (or R = E/I, where E is Electromotive force/Voltage).

questions.question: A knitting needle and a mechanical wood splitter are physical examples of which type of simple machine?
questions.explanation: A wedge is an active tool used to separate two objects or portions of an object, lift an object, or hold an object in place.

questions.question: If a speed reducing system (like belts or gears) has a reduction ratio of 2:1, and exactly 200 RPM is input into the driver, what will the output speed be?
questions.explanation: A 2 to 1 reduction means the output rotates once for every two rotations of the input. 200 / 2 = 100 RPM.

questions.question: Using a spreadsheet for documenting data, tracking project variables, and mapping XYZ coordinates allows the engineer to:
options: Produce tables, graphs, charts, and visual formats easily
questions.explanation: Spreadsheets aggregate raw data and visually extrapolate them into charts and graphs for project analytics and Gantt tracking.

questions.question: When drawing a truss vector array, a truss member reacting to being 'in tension' will experience what physical deformation?
options: It bends at a perfect 90 degree angle
questions.explanation: Tension is a pulling force. When a rod is pulled from both ends, elastic deformation causes it to elongate (longer) and significantly decrease in cross-sectional diameter (skinnier).

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "A safety procedure ensuring dangerous machines are properly shut off and cannot be started up again prior to the completion of maintenance. Requires placing a lock and tag on the energy-isolating device."
questions.explanation: Lock-out/Tag-out (LOTO) is defined as: A safety procedure ensuring dangerous machines are properly shut off and cannot be started up again prior to the completion of maintenance. Requires placing a lock and tag on the energy-isolating device.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "NFPA 70 — a regionally adoptable standard for the safe installation of electrical wiring and equipment. Sets the foundation for electrical safety in residential, commercial, and industrial occupancies."
questions.explanation: NEC (National Electrical Code) is defined as: NFPA 70 — a regionally adoptable standard for the safe installation of electrical wiring and equipment. Sets the foundation for electrical safety in residential, commercial, and industrial occupancies.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Equipment worn to minimize exposure to hazards: safety glasses, gloves, hard hats, steel-toe boots, hearing protection, arc flash suits. Selection depends on the specific hazard."
questions.explanation: PPE (Personal Protective Equipment) is defined as: Equipment worn to minimize exposure to hazards: safety glasses, gloves, hard hats, steel-toe boots, hearing protection, arc flash suits. Selection depends on the specific hazard.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Product identifier, manufacturer/supplier name and contact info, recommended use, and restrictions on use."
questions.explanation: SDS Section 1 — Identification is defined as: Product identifier, manufacturer/supplier name and contact info, recommended use, and restrictions on use.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "All hazards of the chemical and required label elements: signal words, hazard statements, pictograms, and precautionary statements."
questions.explanation: SDS Section 2 — Hazard Identification is defined as: All hazards of the chemical and required label elements: signal words, hazard statements, pictograms, and precautionary statements.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Information on chemical ingredients including chemical name, common name, CAS number, and concentration."
questions.explanation: SDS Section 3 — Composition is defined as: Information on chemical ingredients including chemical name, common name, CAS number, and concentration.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Necessary first-aid instructions by route of exposure (inhalation, skin, eye, ingestion) and most important symptoms/effects."
questions.explanation: SDS Section 4 — First-Aid Measures is defined as: Necessary first-aid instructions by route of exposure (inhalation, skin, eye, ingestion) and most important symptoms/effects.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Suitable extinguishing techniques and equipment, and chemical hazards that develop from fire."
questions.explanation: SDS Section 5 — Fire-Fighting Measures is defined as: Suitable extinguishing techniques and equipment, and chemical hazards that develop from fire.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Exposure limits, engineering controls, and personal protective equipment recommendations."
questions.explanation: SDS Section 8 — Exposure Controls/PPE is defined as: Exposure limits, engineering controls, and personal protective equipment recommendations.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Safety barriers or devices on machinery to protect operators from rotating parts, flying chips, sparks. Required by OSHA for point of operation, nip points, and all rotating parts."
questions.explanation: Machine Guarding is defined as: Safety barriers or devices on machinery to protect operators from rotating parts, flying chips, sparks. Required by OSHA for point of operation, nip points, and all rotating parts.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "OSHA's 'Right to Know' standard requiring employers to inform employees about chemical hazards through labels, SDS sheets, and training programs."
questions.explanation: HazCom (Hazard Communication) is defined as: OSHA's 'Right to Know' standard requiring employers to inform employees about chemical hazards through labels, SDS sheets, and training programs.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Nine standardized symbols used on chemical labels: flame, exploding bomb, oxidizer, gas cylinder, corrosion, skull and crossbones, exclamation mark, health hazard, environment."
questions.explanation: GHS Pictograms is defined as: Nine standardized symbols used on chemical labels: flame, exploding bomb, oxidizer, gas cylinder, corrosion, skull and crossbones, exclamation mark, health hazard, environment.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Disconnecting machinery from ALL energy sources — electrical, mechanical, pneumatic, hydraulic, thermal, chemical — before servicing. Critical part of LOTO."
questions.explanation: Energy Isolation is defined as: Disconnecting machinery from ALL energy sources — electrical, mechanical, pneumatic, hydraulic, thermal, chemical — before servicing. Critical part of LOTO.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "A: ordinary combustibles (wood, paper). B: flammable liquids. C: electrical fires. D: combustible metals. K: cooking oils/fats."
questions.explanation: Fire Extinguisher Classes is defined as: A: ordinary combustibles (wood, paper). B: flammable liquids. C: electrical fires. D: combustible metals. K: cooking oils/fats.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "A multi-purpose dry chemical extinguisher effective on Class A, B, and C fires. Most common type in industrial settings."
questions.explanation: ABC Fire Extinguisher is defined as: A multi-purpose dry chemical extinguisher effective on Class A, B, and C fires. Most common type in industrial settings.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "OSHA requires proper housekeeping, adequate lighting, slip-resistant surfaces. Guardrails at 4+ feet. Ladders follow the 4:1 rule for setup angle."
questions.explanation: Walking and Working Surfaces is defined as: OSHA requires proper housekeeping, adequate lighting, slip-resistant surfaces. Guardrails at 4+ feet. Ladders follow the 4:1 rule for setup angle.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "For every 4 feet of working height, the ladder base should be 1 foot away from the wall. This ensures safe ladder angle (75.5°)."
questions.explanation: 4:1 Ladder Rule is defined as: For every 4 feet of working height, the ladder base should be 1 foot away from the wall. This ensures safe ladder angle (75.5°).

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Do NOT touch the victim while they are in contact with the source. Disconnect power first, then call 911, perform CPR if needed."
questions.explanation: First Aid — Electrical Shock is defined as: Do NOT touch the victim while they are in contact with the source. Disconnect power first, then call 911, perform CPR if needed.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Flush affected area with large amounts of water for at least 15-20 minutes. Remove contaminated clothing. Seek medical attention."
questions.explanation: First Aid — Chemical Burns is defined as: Flush affected area with large amounts of water for at least 15-20 minutes. Remove contaminated clothing. Seek medical attention.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "A device that monitors current imbalance between hot and neutral wires. Trips in milliseconds if current leaks to ground, preventing electrocution. Required in wet locations."
questions.explanation: Ground Fault Circuit Interrupter (GFCI) is defined as: A device that monitors current imbalance between hot and neutral wires. Trips in milliseconds if current leaks to ground, preventing electrocution. Required in wet locations.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "All hazardous waste must be properly labeled, stored in compatible containers, and disposed of through licensed facilities. Never pour chemicals down drains or into regular trash."
questions.explanation: Hazardous Waste Disposal is defined as: All hazardous waste must be properly labeled, stored in compatible containers, and disposed of through licensed facilities. Never pour chemicals down drains or into regular trash.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Step-by-step documents describing how to perform a specific task. More detailed than SOPs and targeted at the operator level. Include safety warnings."
questions.explanation: Work Instructions is defined as: Step-by-step documents describing how to perform a specific task. More detailed than SOPs and targeted at the operator level. Include safety warnings.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Documents recording equipment malfunctions: date, time, symptoms, corrective actions, parts replaced. Used for trend analysis and preventive maintenance planning."
questions.explanation: Equipment Failure Logs is defined as: Documents recording equipment malfunctions: date, time, symptoms, corrective actions, parts replaced. Used for trend analysis and preventive maintenance planning.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Manuals and procedures describing how to start, run, adjust, and shut down equipment. Include safety warnings and normal operating parameters."
questions.explanation: Operating Documents is defined as: Manuals and procedures describing how to start, run, adjust, and shut down equipment. Include safety warnings and normal operating parameters.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Recorded data from equipment testing: measurements, pass/fail results, dates, technician signatures. Used for quality assurance and compliance documentation."
questions.explanation: Test Logs is defined as: Recorded data from equipment testing: measurements, pass/fail results, dates, technician signatures. Used for quality assurance and compliance documentation.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Clear, concise verbal instructions using standard terminology. Critical during troubleshooting — technicians must accurately describe symptoms and relay findings to supervisors."
questions.explanation: Verbal Communication in Maintenance is defined as: Clear, concise verbal instructions using standard terminology. Critical during troubleshooting — technicians must accurately describe symptoms and relay findings to supervisors.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "A documented plan for regular equipment inspections, cleaning, lubrication, and parts replacement to prevent unexpected breakdowns and extend equipment life."
questions.explanation: Preventive Maintenance Schedule is defined as: A documented plan for regular equipment inspections, cleaning, lubrication, and parts replacement to prevent unexpected breakdowns and extend equipment life.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Documented step-by-step instructions for routine operations. Ensure consistency, safety, and quality across all shifts and operators."
questions.explanation: Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) is defined as: Documented step-by-step instructions for routine operations. Ensure consistency, safety, and quality across all shifts and operators.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "A document that records the problem, diagnostic steps taken, root cause identified, repair performed, and verification that the issue is resolved."
questions.explanation: Troubleshooting Report is defined as: A document that records the problem, diagnostic steps taken, root cause identified, repair performed, and verification that the issue is resolved.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Displays voltage waveforms over time on a screen. Used to analyze signal shape, frequency, amplitude, and timing relationships. Essential for troubleshooting AC and digital signals."
questions.explanation: Oscilloscope is defined as: Displays voltage waveforms over time on a screen. Used to analyze signal shape, frequency, amplitude, and timing relationships. Essential for troubleshooting AC and digital signals.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Most common pressure gauge type. A curved metal tube straightens under pressure, moving a pointer through a mechanical linkage. Reads in PSI, bar, or kPa."
questions.explanation: Pressure Gauge — Bourdon Tube is defined as: Most common pressure gauge type. A curved metal tube straightens under pressure, moving a pointer through a mechanical linkage. Reads in PSI, bar, or kPa.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Turbine (spinning blades), Ultrasonic (sound waves), Magnetic (electromagnetic induction for conductive fluids), Vortex (measures turbulence frequency)."
questions.explanation: Flow Sensor Types is defined as: Turbine (spinning blades), Ultrasonic (sound waves), Magnetic (electromagnetic induction for conductive fluids), Vortex (measures turbulence frequency).

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Temperature sensor where resistance changes with temperature. Made of platinum (Pt100 is most common). More accurate and stable than thermocouples but slower response."
questions.explanation: RTD (Resistance Temperature Detector) is defined as: Temperature sensor where resistance changes with temperature. Made of platinum (Pt100 is most common). More accurate and stable than thermocouples but slower response.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "A temperature-sensitive resistor. NTC (Negative Temperature Coefficient): resistance decreases with heat. PTC: resistance increases with heat. Very sensitive but limited range."
questions.explanation: Thermistor is defined as: A temperature-sensitive resistor. NTC (Negative Temperature Coefficient): resistance decreases with heat. PTC: resistance increases with heat. Very sensitive but limited range.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Detects metal objects without physical contact using electromagnetic fields. Only works with ferrous and non-ferrous metals. Range typically 1-30mm."
questions.explanation: Proximity Sensor — Inductive is defined as: Detects metal objects without physical contact using electromagnetic fields. Only works with ferrous and non-ferrous metals. Range typically 1-30mm.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Detects any material (metal, plastic, liquid, powder) using changes in capacitance. Useful for level detection through non-metallic container walls."
questions.explanation: Proximity Sensor — Capacitive is defined as: Detects any material (metal, plastic, liquid, powder) using changes in capacitance. Useful for level detection through non-metallic container walls.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Magnetically activated switch with two thin metal reeds in a glass tube. Closes when a magnet approaches. Used for position sensing and counting."
questions.explanation: Reed Switch/Sensor is defined as: Magnetically activated switch with two thin metal reeds in a glass tube. Closes when a magnet approaches. Used for position sensing and counting.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Measures shaft rotation. Incremental: outputs pulses per revolution. Absolute: outputs unique position code. Used for motor speed and position feedback in servo systems."
questions.explanation: Encoder — Rotary is defined as: Measures shaft rotation. Incremental: outputs pulses per revolution. Absolute: outputs unique position code. Used for motor speed and position feedback in servo systems.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Industrial standard for analog signal transmission. 4 mA = 0% (minimum), 20 mA = 100% (maximum). 4 mA baseline allows detection of broken wires (0 mA = fault)."
questions.explanation: 4-20 mA Signal is defined as: Industrial standard for analog signal transmission. 4 mA = 0% (minimum), 20 mA = 100% (maximum). 4 mA baseline allows detection of broken wires (0 mA = fault).

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Precision instrument measuring to 0.001 inch or 0.01 mm. Uses a calibrated screw mechanism. Types: outside (OD), inside (ID), and depth micrometers."
questions.explanation: Micrometer is defined as: Precision instrument measuring to 0.001 inch or 0.01 mm. Uses a calibrated screw mechanism. Types: outside (OD), inside (ID), and depth micrometers.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Measures inside, outside, depth, and step dimensions. Vernier scale reads to 0.001 inch. Digital calipers provide direct readout. Range typically 0-6 inches."
questions.explanation: Vernier Caliper is defined as: Measures inside, outside, depth, and step dimensions. Vernier scale reads to 0.001 inch. Digital calipers provide direct readout. Range typically 0-6 inches.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Weighted tool on a string establishing true vertical reference. Gravity ensures the string hangs perfectly straight."
questions.explanation: Plumb Bob is defined as: Weighted tool on a string establishing true vertical reference. Gravity ensures the string hangs perfectly straight.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Versatile layout tool for checking 90° and 45° angles, measuring depth, and scribing lines. Has a ruler, square head, protractor head, and center head."
questions.explanation: Combination Square is defined as: Versatile layout tool for checking 90° and 45° angles, measuring depth, and scribing lines. Has a ruler, square head, protractor head, and center head.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Accuracy: how close to the true value. Precision: how repeatable the measurement is. An instrument can be precise but not accurate (consistently wrong). Calibration fixes accuracy."
questions.explanation: Accuracy vs. Precision is defined as: Accuracy: how close to the true value. Precision: how repeatable the measurement is. An instrument can be precise but not accurate (consistently wrong). Calibration fixes accuracy.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Opposition to current flow. Measured in ohms (Ω). Like a restriction in a pipe reducing water flow."
questions.explanation: Resistance (R) is defined as: Opposition to current flow. Measured in ohms (Ω). Like a restriction in a pipe reducing water flow.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "The sum of all voltages around any closed loop equals zero. Voltage supplied equals total voltage dropped across all components."
questions.explanation: Kirchhoff's Voltage Law (KVL) is defined as: The sum of all voltages around any closed loop equals zero. Voltage supplied equals total voltage dropped across all components.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Total current entering a junction equals total current leaving. Current is conserved at any node in a circuit."
questions.explanation: Kirchhoff's Current Law (KCL) is defined as: Total current entering a junction equals total current leaving. Current is conserved at any node in a circuit.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "A combination circuit with some components in series and others in parallel. Analyze by simplifying parallel sections first, then treat as series."
questions.explanation: Series-Parallel Circuit is defined as: A combination circuit with some components in series and others in parallel. Analyze by simplifying parallel sections first, then treat as series.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "SI unit of electrical charge. 1 coulomb ≈ 6.24 × 10^18 electrons. When 1 coulomb passes a point in 1 second, the current is 1 ampere."
questions.explanation: Coulomb is defined as: SI unit of electrical charge. 1 coulomb ≈ 6.24 × 10^18 electrons. When 1 coulomb passes a point in 1 second, the current is 1 ampere.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Unit of energy. 1 kWh = using 1000 watts for 1 hour. This is what electric utilities bill for. Energy = Power × Time."
questions.explanation: Kilowatt-hour (kWh) is defined as: Unit of energy. 1 kWh = using 1000 watts for 1 hour. This is what electric utilities bill for. Energy = Power × Time.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Black=0, Brown=1, Red=2, Orange=3, Yellow=4, Green=5, Blue=6, Violet=7, Gray=8, White=9. Band 1&2 = digits, Band 3 = multiplier, Band 4 = tolerance."
questions.explanation: Resistor Color Code is defined as: Black=0, Brown=1, Red=2, Orange=3, Yellow=4, Green=5, Blue=6, Violet=7, Gray=8, White=9. Band 1&2 = digits, Band 3 = multiplier, Band 4 = tolerance.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Gold = ±5%, Silver = ±10%, No band = ±20%. Tolerance indicates how close actual resistance is to the stated value."
questions.explanation: Tolerance Bands is defined as: Gold = ±5%, Silver = ±10%, No band = ±20%. Tolerance indicates how close actual resistance is to the stated value.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Current periodically reverses direction. US standard: 120V at 60 Hz. Industrial: 208V, 480V 3-phase. Used for power transmission — voltage easily transformed."
questions.explanation: AC (Alternating Current) is defined as: Current periodically reverses direction. US standard: 120V at 60 Hz. Industrial: 208V, 480V 3-phase. Used for power transmission — voltage easily transformed.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Current flows in one direction only. From batteries, solar cells, and rectified AC. Used in electronics, PLCs, and control circuits."
questions.explanation: DC (Direct Current) is defined as: Current flows in one direction only. From batteries, solar cells, and rectified AC. Used in electronics, PLCs, and control circuits.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Transfers electrical energy between circuits through electromagnetic induction. Steps voltage up or down. Turns ratio: Vp/Vs = Np/Ns. Only works with AC."
questions.explanation: Transformer is defined as: Transfers electrical energy between circuits through electromagnetic induction. Steps voltage up or down. Turns ratio: Vp/Vs = Np/Ns. Only works with AC.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Secondary has fewer turns than primary — reduces voltage, increases current. Example: 480V to 120V for control circuits."
questions.explanation: Step-Down Transformer is defined as: Secondary has fewer turns than primary — reduces voltage, increases current. Example: 480V to 120V for control circuits.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Secondary has more turns than primary — increases voltage, reduces current. Used in power transmission to reduce line losses."
questions.explanation: Step-Up Transformer is defined as: Secondary has more turns than primary — increases voltage, reduces current. Used in power transmission to reduce line losses.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Converts AC to DC. Half-wave uses one diode (50% of cycle). Full-wave bridge uses four diodes (100% of cycle). Output is pulsating DC — smoothed by a capacitor."
questions.explanation: Rectifier is defined as: Converts AC to DC. Half-wave uses one diode (50% of cycle). Full-wave bridge uses four diodes (100% of cycle). Output is pulsating DC — smoothed by a capacitor.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Converts DC to AC. Variable Frequency Drive controls motor speed by varying output frequency and voltage. Saves energy, provides soft start, and precise speed control."
questions.explanation: Inverter / VFD is defined as: Converts DC to AC. Variable Frequency Drive controls motor speed by varying output frequency and voltage. Saves energy, provides soft start, and precise speed control.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Converts mechanical energy to electrical energy through electromagnetic induction. Rotating conductor in a magnetic field produces voltage (Faraday's Law)."
questions.explanation: Generator is defined as: Converts mechanical energy to electrical energy through electromagnetic induction. Rotating conductor in a magnetic field produces voltage (Faraday's Law).

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Standard control voltage in industrial automation. Steps down and rectifies line voltage for safe, low-voltage DC power for sensors, PLCs, and control devices."
questions.explanation: 24V DC Power Supply is defined as: Standard control voltage in industrial automation. Steps down and rectifies line voltage for safe, low-voltage DC power for sensors, PLCs, and control devices.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "A contactor combined with an overload relay. Provides motor control (start/stop) and protection against overcurrent conditions."
questions.explanation: Motor Starter is defined as: A contactor combined with an overload relay. Provides motor control (start/stop) and protection against overcurrent conditions.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "A resettable overcurrent protection device. Trips open on overcurrent, can be reset to restore the circuit. More convenient than fuses for frequent operations."
questions.explanation: Circuit Breaker is defined as: A resettable overcurrent protection device. Trips open on overcurrent, can be reset to restore the circuit. More convenient than fuses for frequent operations.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Three-terminal variable resistor providing adjustable voltage output. Acts as a voltage divider. Used for volume controls, position feedback, setpoint adjustment."
questions.explanation: Potentiometer is defined as: Three-terminal variable resistor providing adjustable voltage output. Acts as a voltage divider. Used for volume controls, position feedback, setpoint adjustment.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Stores electrical energy in a magnetic field when current flows through a coil. Measured in Henrys (H). Opposes changes in current. Used in filters and electromagnetic devices."
questions.explanation: Inductor is defined as: Stores electrical energy in a magnetic field when current flows through a coil. Measured in Henrys (H). Opposes changes in current. Used in filters and electromagnetic devices.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Light-Emitting Diode. A semiconductor that converts electrical energy directly to light. Low power, long life. Used as indicators in control panels and circuit boards."
questions.explanation: LED is defined as: Light-Emitting Diode. A semiconductor that converts electrical energy directly to light. Low power, long life. Used as indicators in control panels and circuit boards.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Motors use interaction between magnetic fields (from permanent magnets or electromagnets) to create rotational force. Current-carrying conductor in a magnetic field experiences a force (Lorentz)."
questions.explanation: Magnetism in Motors is defined as: Motors use interaction between magnetic fields (from permanent magnets or electromagnets) to create rotational force. Current-carrying conductor in a magnetic field experiences a force (Lorentz).

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Shows electrical connections and functions using standardized symbols. Shows logical relationships, not physical layout."
questions.explanation: Schematic Diagram is defined as: Shows electrical connections and functions using standardized symbols. Shows logical relationships, not physical layout.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Shows physical connections and layout — actual wire routing, terminal locations, and component placement. Used for installation and maintenance."
questions.explanation: Wiring Diagram is defined as: Shows physical connections and layout — actual wire routing, terminal locations, and component placement. Used for installation and maintenance.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "American Wire Gauge. LOWER number = THICKER wire = MORE current. 14 AWG = 15A, 12 AWG = 20A, 10 AWG = 30A, 8 AWG = 40A."
questions.explanation: Wire Gauge (AWG) is defined as: American Wire Gauge. LOWER number = THICKER wire = MORE current. 14 AWG = 15A, 12 AWG = 20A, 10 AWG = 30A, 8 AWG = 40A.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Red = positive (+), Black = negative (−), Green or bare copper = ground. In AC: Black = hot, White = neutral, Green = ground."
questions.explanation: Wire Color Code (DC) is defined as: Red = positive (+), Black = negative (−), Green or bare copper = ground. In AC: Black = hot, White = neutral, Green = ground.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Connecting equipment frames and enclosures to earth ground for safety. Provides a low-resistance path for fault current, causing protective devices to trip quickly."
questions.explanation: Grounding is defined as: Connecting equipment frames and enclosures to earth ground for safety. Provides a low-resistance path for fault current, causing protective devices to trip quickly.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Three AC voltages 120° apart. More efficient for motors and heavy loads. Delta and Wye configurations. Common industrial voltages: 208V, 480V."
questions.explanation: 3-Phase Power is defined as: Three AC voltages 120° apart. More efficient for motors and heavy loads. Delta and Wye configurations. Common industrial voltages: 208V, 480V.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Requires a starting mechanism (capacitor or split-phase winding) because single-phase AC alone produces no starting torque. Used for residential/light commercial."
questions.explanation: Single-Phase Motor is defined as: Requires a starting mechanism (capacitor or split-phase winding) because single-phase AC alone produces no starting torque. Used for residential/light commercial.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Self-starting — three phases naturally create a rotating magnetic field. More efficient, smoother operation than single-phase. Standard in industrial applications."
questions.explanation: Three-Phase Motor is defined as: Self-starting — three phases naturally create a rotating magnetic field. More efficient, smoother operation than single-phase. Standard in industrial applications.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Ferrous = contains iron (steel, cast iron) — magnetic, prone to rust. Non-Ferrous = no iron (aluminum, copper, brass) — non-magnetic, corrosion resistant."
questions.explanation: Ferrous vs. Non-Ferrous Metals is defined as: Ferrous = contains iron (steel, cast iron) — magnetic, prone to rust. Non-Ferrous = no iron (aluminum, copper, brass) — non-magnetic, corrosion resistant.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Iron alloy with minimum 10.5% chromium. Highly corrosion resistant. Types: 304 (general), 316 (marine/chemical). Used in food processing, medical, harsh environments."
questions.explanation: Stainless Steel is defined as: Iron alloy with minimum 10.5% chromium. Highly corrosion resistant. Types: 304 (general), 316 (marine/chemical). Used in food processing, medical, harsh environments.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Thermoplastics: can be re-melted and reshaped (nylon, PVC, polyethylene). Thermosets: permanent shape after curing, cannot be re-melted (epoxy, phenolic, polyester)."
questions.explanation: Thermoplastics vs. Thermosets is defined as: Thermoplastics: can be re-melted and reshaped (nylon, PVC, polyethylene). Thermosets: permanent shape after curing, cannot be re-melted (epoxy, phenolic, polyester).

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "OSHA limit: 90 dBA for 8-hour TWA. Controls: engineering (enclosures, barriers), administrative (job rotation, scheduling), PPE (earplugs rated NRR 25-33)."
questions.explanation: Noise Control is defined as: OSHA limit: 90 dBA for 8-hour TWA. Controls: engineering (enclosures, barriers), administrative (job rotation, scheduling), PPE (earplugs rated NRR 25-33).

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Contains hardened steel balls between inner and outer races. Handles radial and light thrust loads. Most common bearing type. Sizes standardized by ABMA."
questions.explanation: Ball Bearing is defined as: Contains hardened steel balls between inner and outer races. Handles radial and light thrust loads. Most common bearing type. Sizes standardized by ABMA.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Uses cylindrical rollers for heavy radial loads. Higher load capacity than ball bearings of same size. Cannot handle significant thrust loads."
questions.explanation: Roller Bearing — Cylindrical is defined as: Uses cylindrical rollers for heavy radial loads. Higher load capacity than ball bearings of same size. Cannot handle significant thrust loads.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Tapered rollers handle combined heavy radial AND thrust loads. Used in wheel hubs, gearboxes. Always installed in opposing pairs."
questions.explanation: Roller Bearing — Tapered is defined as: Tapered rollers handle combined heavy radial AND thrust loads. Used in wheel hubs, gearboxes. Always installed in opposing pairs.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Self-aligning bearings with barrel-shaped rollers. Accommodate misalignment. Used where shaft deflection or misaligned housings are expected."
questions.explanation: Roller Bearing — Spherical is defined as: Self-aligning bearings with barrel-shaped rollers. Accommodate misalignment. Used where shaft deflection or misaligned housings are expected.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Long, thin rollers for applications with limited radial space. High load capacity relative to size. Common in automotive transmissions."
questions.explanation: Roller Bearing — Needle is defined as: Long, thin rollers for applications with limited radial space. High load capacity relative to size. Common in automotive transmissions.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Simple bushings with sliding contact (no rolling elements). For low speed, high load, or oscillating motion. Require lubrication film. Made of bronze, babbit, or polymer."
questions.explanation: Plain (Sleeve/Journal) Bearing is defined as: Simple bushings with sliding contact (no rolling elements). For low speed, high load, or oscillating motion. Require lubrication film. Made of bronze, babbit, or polymer.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "#1: Contamination. #2: Improper lubrication. #3: Misalignment. #4: Overloading. #5: Improper installation. Regular inspection and proper maintenance prevents most failures."
questions.explanation: Bearing Failure Causes is defined as: #1: Contamination. #2: Improper lubrication. #3: Misalignment. #4: Overloading. #5: Improper installation. Regular inspection and proper maintenance prevents most failures.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Creates a film between surfaces to reduce friction, dissipate heat, and prevent corrosion. Grease: most bearings. Oil: high speed. Proper amount is critical — over-greasing causes heat."
questions.explanation: Bearing Lubrication is defined as: Creates a film between surfaces to reduce friction, dissipate heat, and prevent corrosion. Grease: most bearings. Oil: high speed. Proper amount is critical — over-greasing causes heat.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Shaft centerlines are parallel but offset. Causes twice-per-revolution vibration in the radial direction."
questions.explanation: Parallel (Offset) Misalignment is defined as: Shaft centerlines are parallel but offset. Causes twice-per-revolution vibration in the radial direction.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Teeth cut at an angle to the shaft. Quieter and smoother than spur gears, handle higher loads. Produce axial thrust — require thrust bearings."
questions.explanation: Helical Gear is defined as: Teeth cut at an angle to the shaft. Quieter and smoother than spur gears, handle higher loads. Produce axial thrust — require thrust bearings.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Cone-shaped gears for shafts that intersect (usually 90°). Spiral bevel gears are smoother. Used in differentials and right-angle drives."
questions.explanation: Bevel Gear is defined as: Cone-shaped gears for shafts that intersect (usually 90°). Spiral bevel gears are smoother. Used in differentials and right-angle drives.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "RPM_out = RPM_in × (Teeth_driving / Teeth_driven). Example: 1800 RPM × (20/60) = 600 RPM output."
questions.explanation: Gear Speed Calculation is defined as: RPM_out = RPM_in × (Teeth_driving / Teeth_driven). Example: 1800 RPM × (20/60) = 600 RPM output.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "RPM_driven = RPM_driver × (Diameter_driver / Diameter_driven). Small driving large = speed reduction & torque increase."
questions.explanation: Pulley/Sheave Speed Calculation is defined as: RPM_driven = RPM_driver × (Diameter_driver / Diameter_driven). Small driving large = speed reduction & torque increase.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Most common belt type. V-shape wedges into pulley groove for better grip. Slight slip is normal (1-3%). Multiple belts for higher loads. Replace in matched sets."
questions.explanation: V-Belt Drive is defined as: Most common belt type. V-shape wedges into pulley groove for better grip. Slight slip is normal (1-3%). Multiple belts for higher loads. Replace in matched sets.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Toothed belt meshing with toothed pulleys. Zero slip — maintains exact speed ratio. Used where precise timing is critical (engine timing, CNC machines)."
questions.explanation: Synchronous (Timing) Belt is defined as: Toothed belt meshing with toothed pulleys. Zero slip — maintains exact speed ratio. Used where precise timing is critical (engine timing, CNC machines).

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Roller chain on sprockets. Positive drive (no slip). For heavy loads and harsh environments. Requires lubrication and periodic tension adjustment."
questions.explanation: Chain Drive is defined as: Roller chain on sprockets. Positive drive (no slip). For heavy loads and harsh environments. Requires lubrication and periodic tension adjustment.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Rigid: no misalignment tolerance (flanged). Flexible: accommodates slight misalignment (jaw, gear, disc). Fluid: uses hydraulic fluid for smooth power transfer."
questions.explanation: Coupling Types is defined as: Rigid: no misalignment tolerance (flanged). Flexible: accommodates slight misalignment (jaw, gear, disc). Fluid: uses hydraulic fluid for smooth power transfer.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Belt: general material handling. Roller: gravity or powered for boxes/pallets. Chain: heavy items. Screw: bulk materials (auger). Pneumatic: powders/granules through tubes."
questions.explanation: Conveyor Types is defined as: Belt: general material handling. Roller: gravity or powered for boxes/pallets. Chain: heavy items. Screw: bulk materials (auger). Pneumatic: powders/granules through tubes.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Grade 2: low carbon, general use. Grade 5: medium carbon, most common. Grade 8: alloy steel, high strength. Higher grade = more marks on head = stronger."
questions.explanation: Fastener Bolt Grades is defined as: Grade 2: low carbon, general use. Grade 5: medium carbon, most common. Grade 8: alloy steel, high strength. Higher grade = more marks on head = stronger.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "UNC (coarse): fast assembly, general purpose. UNF (fine): precision, vibration resistance. Metric: M + diameter × pitch (M10×1.5)."
questions.explanation: Thread Types is defined as: UNC (coarse): fast assembly, general purpose. UNF (fine): precision, vibration resistance. Metric: M + diameter × pitch (M10×1.5).

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Rectangular keys fit into keyways on shafts and hubs to prevent rotation. Types: square, rectangular, Woodruff (half-moon). Transmit torque between shaft and component."
questions.explanation: Keys and Keyways is defined as: Rectangular keys fit into keyways on shafts and hubs to prevent rotation. Types: square, rectangular, Woodruff (half-moon). Transmit torque between shaft and component.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "A screw that threads entirely into one part to secure it against another (usually shaft to hub). Types: flat, cone, cup, half-dog point."
questions.explanation: Set Screw is defined as: A screw that threads entirely into one part to secure it against another (usually shaft to hub). Types: flat, cone, cup, half-dog point.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Snap rings that fit into grooves on shafts (external) or bores (internal) to axially position components. Installed/removed with snap ring pliers."
questions.explanation: Retaining Rings is defined as: Snap rings that fit into grooves on shafts (external) or bores (internal) to axially position components. Installed/removed with snap ring pliers.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Seals: precision-machined faces, low friction, low leakage. Packing: braided rope compressed by a gland follower, adjustable, allows slight leakage for cooling."
questions.explanation: Mechanical Seal vs. Packing is defined as: Seals: precision-machined faces, low friction, low leakage. Packing: braided rope compressed by a gland follower, adjustable, allows slight leakage for cooling.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Shows internal features by cutting through the object. Crosshatching indicates cut solid material. Half-sections show both internal and external features."
questions.explanation: Sectional View (Drawing) is defined as: Shows internal features by cutting through the object. Crosshatching indicates cut solid material. Half-sections show both internal and external features.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "2:1 = drawing is twice actual size (enlarged). 1:1 = full size. 1:2 = half actual size (reduced). Always noted in the title block."
questions.explanation: Scale in Drawings is defined as: 2:1 = drawing is twice actual size (enlarged). 1:1 = full size. 1:2 = half actual size (reduced). Always noted in the title block.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "A system of symbols specifying allowable variations in form, profile, orientation, location, and runout. More precise than conventional tolerancing."
questions.explanation: GD&T (Geometric Dimensioning and Tolerancing) is defined as: A system of symbols specifying allowable variations in form, profile, orientation, location, and runout. More precise than conventional tolerancing.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Oil: flows freely, good for high speed, cooling ability, enclosed systems. Grease: stays in place, good for slow speed, open bearings. Selection based on speed, load, temperature."
questions.explanation: Lubrication — Oil vs. Grease is defined as: Oil: flows freely, good for high speed, cooling ability, enclosed systems. Grease: stays in place, good for slow speed, open bearings. Selection based on speed, load, temperature.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Uses pressurized LIQUID (oil) to transmit force. Incompressible fluid provides precise control. Operates at high pressures (up to 5000+ PSI). Used for heavy-duty applications."
questions.explanation: Hydraulic System is defined as: Uses pressurized LIQUID (oil) to transmit force. Incompressible fluid provides precise control. Operates at high pressures (up to 5000+ PSI). Used for heavy-duty applications.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Uses compressed AIR to transmit force. Air is compressible — less precise than hydraulics. Operates 80-120 PSI typically. Cleaner, faster, easier to maintain."
questions.explanation: Pneumatic System is defined as: Uses compressed AIR to transmit force. Air is compressible — less precise than hydraulics. Operates 80-120 PSI typically. Cleaner, faster, easier to maintain.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Hydraulic: incompressible, more force, precise, oil can leak/contaminate. Pneumatic: compressible, less force, faster cycles, clean (air), spring-like action due to compressibility."
questions.explanation: Hydraulic vs. Pneumatic Key Differences is defined as: Hydraulic: incompressible, more force, precise, oil can leak/contaminate. Pneumatic: compressible, less force, faster cycles, clean (air), spring-like action due to compressibility.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Force = Pressure × Area. A 2-inch diameter cylinder at 1000 PSI: Area = π(1)² = 3.14 in². Force = 1000 × 3.14 = 3140 lbs."
questions.explanation: Hydraulic Force Calculation is defined as: Force = Pressure × Area. A 2-inch diameter cylinder at 1000 PSI: Area = π(1)² = 3.14 in². Force = 1000 × 3.14 = 3140 lbs.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Maintains a constant lower pressure in a branch circuit regardless of main system pressure."
questions.explanation: Pressure Reducing Valve is defined as: Maintains a constant lower pressure in a branch circuit regardless of main system pressure.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Controls fluid flow rate, which determines actuator SPEED. Needle valve is the most basic type. Pressure-compensated types maintain constant speed regardless of load changes."
questions.explanation: Flow Control Valve is defined as: Controls fluid flow rate, which determines actuator SPEED. Needle valve is the most basic type. Pressure-compensated types maintain constant speed regardless of load changes.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Controls the PATH of fluid flow — directs actuator movement. Designated by positions and ways: 4/3 = 4 ports, 3 positions. Operated by solenoid, lever, or pilot pressure."
questions.explanation: Directional Control Valve (DCV) is defined as: Controls the PATH of fluid flow — directs actuator movement. Designated by positions and ways: 4/3 = 4 ports, 3 positions. Operated by solenoid, lever, or pilot pressure.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Fluid pressure in ONE direction only (usually extend). Returns by spring force or gravity. One port. Simple and cost-effective."
questions.explanation: Single-Acting Cylinder is defined as: Fluid pressure in ONE direction only (usually extend). Returns by spring force or gravity. One port. Simple and cost-effective.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Fluid pressure in BOTH directions — extends and retracts under power. Two ports. Most common type. Retract force is less (rod reduces area)."
questions.explanation: Double-Acting Cylinder is defined as: Fluid pressure in BOTH directions — extends and retracts under power. Two ports. Most common type. Retract force is less (rod reduces area).

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Converts hydraulic energy into continuous rotary motion. Types: gear, vane, piston. Like a pump in reverse — fluid in, rotation out."
questions.explanation: Hydraulic Motor is defined as: Converts hydraulic energy into continuous rotary motion. Types: gear, vane, piston. Like a pump in reverse — fluid in, rotation out.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "External gear pump: two meshing gears trap fluid between teeth and housing. Simple, rugged, low cost. Fixed displacement. Most common hydraulic pump type."
questions.explanation: Gear Pump is defined as: External gear pump: two meshing gears trap fluid between teeth and housing. Simple, rugged, low cost. Fixed displacement. Most common hydraulic pump type.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Sliding vanes in a slotted rotor sweep fluid from inlet to outlet. Quieter than gear pumps. Can be variable displacement (adjustable eccentricity)."
questions.explanation: Vane Pump is defined as: Sliding vanes in a slotted rotor sweep fluid from inlet to outlet. Quieter than gear pumps. Can be variable displacement (adjustable eccentricity).

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Pistons reciprocate in a cylinder block. Highest pressure and efficiency of all pump types. Axial or radial configuration. Variable displacement available."
options: Hydraulic Hose vs. Tube vs. Pipe
questions.explanation: Piston Pump is defined as: Pistons reciprocate in a cylinder block. Highest pressure and efficiency of all pump types. Axial or radial configuration. Variable displacement available.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "A hydraulic pump generates flow (GPM). Pressure is a result of resistance to that flow in the system. No resistance = no pressure buildup. Load creates pressure."
questions.explanation: Pumps Create FLOW, Not Pressure is defined as: A hydraulic pump generates flow (GPM). Pressure is a result of resistance to that flow in the system. No resistance = no pressure buildup. Load creates pressure.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Must be correct viscosity for the operating temperature range. Too thin: internal leakage, wear. Too thick: sluggish, hard starting, cavitation. Viscosity Index indicates temperature stability."
questions.explanation: Hydraulic Fluid — Viscosity is defined as: Must be correct viscosity for the operating temperature range. Too thin: internal leakage, wear. Too thick: sluggish, hard starting, cavitation. Viscosity Index indicates temperature stability.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Filter-Regulator-Lubricator: conditions compressed air before pneumatic devices. Filter removes particles/moisture (5 micron typical). Regulator sets pressure. Lubricator adds oil mist."
questions.explanation: FRL Unit is defined as: Filter-Regulator-Lubricator: conditions compressed air before pneumatic devices. Filter removes particles/moisture (5 micron typical). Regulator sets pressure. Lubricator adds oil mist.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Reciprocating (piston): most common for small shops. Rotary screw: continuous duty, industrial. Centrifugal: very high volume. All require receiver tanks and regular draining."
questions.explanation: Air Compressor Types is defined as: Reciprocating (piston): most common for small shops. Rotary screw: continuous duty, industrial. Centrifugal: very high volume. All require receiver tanks and regular draining.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Circle = pump/motor. Rectangle = cylinder. Diamond = filter. Arrow through symbol = variable. Spring = return mechanism. Lines = conductors/pipes."
questions.explanation: Hydraulic Schematic Symbols is defined as: Circle = pump/motor. Rectangle = cylinder. Diamond = filter. Arrow through symbol = variable. Spring = return mechanism. Lines = conductors/pipes.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Removes contaminants causing wear and failure. Locations: suction line, pressure line, return line. Filter rating in microns (smaller = finer). Contamination is #1 cause of hydraulic failure."
questions.explanation: System Filtration is defined as: Removes contaminants causing wear and failure. Locations: suction line, pressure line, return line. Filter rating in microns (smaller = finer). Contamination is #1 cause of hydraulic failure.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Hose: flexible, for moving parts, pressure rated, annual inspection. Tube: semi-rigid, bendable, clean appearance. Pipe: rigid, permanent installations, threaded fittings."
options: Hydraulic Hose vs. Tube vs. Pipe
questions.explanation: Hydraulic Hose vs. Tube vs. Pipe is defined as: Hose: flexible, for moving parts, pressure rated, annual inspection. Tube: semi-rigid, bendable, clean appearance. Pipe: rigid, permanent installations, threaded fittings.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Industrial computer that continuously monitors inputs, executes stored program logic, and controls outputs. Replaced hardwired relay logic. Main components: CPU, I/O modules, power supply."
questions.explanation: PLC (Programmable Logic Controller) is defined as: Industrial computer that continuously monitors inputs, executes stored program logic, and controls outputs. Replaced hardwired relay logic. Main components: CPU, I/O modules, power supply.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Repetitive process: 1) Read all inputs, 2) Execute program logic top-to-bottom, 3) Update all outputs, 4) Housekeeping/communication. Typical scan: 1-25 milliseconds."
questions.explanation: PLC Scan Cycle is defined as: Repetitive process: 1) Read all inputs, 2) Execute program logic top-to-bottom, 3) Update all outputs, 4) Housekeeping/communication. Typical scan: 1-25 milliseconds.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Interface between field devices and PLC. Convert sensor signals to logic levels. Types: discrete (on/off), analog (4-20mA, 0-10V). Examples: pushbuttons, limit switches, sensors."
questions.explanation: PLC Input Modules is defined as: Interface between field devices and PLC. Convert sensor signals to logic levels. Types: discrete (on/off), analog (4-20mA, 0-10V). Examples: pushbuttons, limit switches, sensors.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Interface between PLC and field devices. Convert logic levels to signals that control devices. Types: relay, transistor (DC), triac (AC). Examples: motors, solenoids, indicator lights."
questions.explanation: PLC Output Modules is defined as: Interface between PLC and field devices. Convert logic levels to signals that control devices. Types: relay, transistor (DC), triac (AC). Examples: motors, solenoids, indicator lights.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "PLC programming language resembling relay ladder diagrams. Contacts (inputs) on the left, coils (outputs) on the right. Rungs evaluated top to bottom, left to right."
questions.explanation: Ladder Logic Programming is defined as: PLC programming language resembling relay ladder diagrams. Contacts (inputs) on the left, coils (outputs) on the right. Rungs evaluated top to bottom, left to right.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Output is TRUE only when ALL inputs are TRUE. Like switches in SERIES. Boolean: Y = A · B. Truth table: only 1·1 = 1."
questions.explanation: AND Logic Gate is defined as: Output is TRUE only when ALL inputs are TRUE. Like switches in SERIES. Boolean: Y = A · B. Truth table: only 1·1 = 1.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Output is TRUE when ANY input is TRUE. Like switches in PARALLEL. Boolean: Y = A + B. Truth table: 0+0 = 0; all others = 1."
questions.explanation: OR Logic Gate is defined as: Output is TRUE when ANY input is TRUE. Like switches in PARALLEL. Boolean: Y = A + B. Truth table: 0+0 = 0; all others = 1.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "AND followed by NOT. Output is FALSE only when ALL inputs are TRUE. Most versatile — any logic function can be built from NAND gates alone."
questions.explanation: NAND Gate is defined as: AND followed by NOT. Output is FALSE only when ALL inputs are TRUE. Most versatile — any logic function can be built from NAND gates alone.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Exclusive OR. Output is TRUE when inputs are DIFFERENT (one high, one low). Output FALSE when both inputs are the same."
questions.explanation: XOR Gate is defined as: Exclusive OR. Output is TRUE when inputs are DIFFERENT (one high, one low). Output FALSE when both inputs are the same.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Base-2 system using only 0 and 1. Each position represents a power of 2. Binary 1010 = 8+0+2+0 = 10 decimal. Foundation of all digital systems."
questions.explanation: Binary Number System is defined as: Base-2 system using only 0 and 1. Each position represents a power of 2. Binary 1010 = 8+0+2+0 = 10 decimal. Foundation of all digital systems.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Software for creating precision 2D drawings and 3D models. Allows easy modification, dimensioning, simulation, and sharing. Examples: AutoCAD, SolidWorks, Fusion 360."
questions.explanation: CAD (Computer-Aided Design) is defined as: Software for creating precision 2D drawings and 3D models. Allows easy modification, dimensioning, simulation, and sharing. Examples: AutoCAD, SolidWorks, Fusion 360.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "User interface connecting operators to machines/processes. Displays real-time data, alarms, trends, and controls. Modern HMIs are touchscreen panels."
questions.explanation: HMI (Human-Machine Interface) is defined as: User interface connecting operators to machines/processes. Displays real-time data, alarms, trends, and controls. Modern HMIs are touchscreen panels.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Standard network cabling using twisted pairs. Cat5e: 1 Gbps. Cat6: 10 Gbps (short runs). RJ-45 connectors. Maximum run: 100 meters (328 feet)."

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Single conductor surrounded by insulation and braided shield. Used for older networks (10Base2, 10Base5), cable TV, and some instrumentation."

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Router: connects different networks, makes routing decisions. Switch: connects devices within a network, directs traffic. Hub: broadcasts to all (obsolete). Server: provides shared services."

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "EtherNet/IP: Ethernet-based, real-time. Modbus: simple, serial or TCP. Profinet: Siemens standard. DeviceNet: CAN-based. Selection depends on speed, distance, and device compatibility."

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Wireless: flexible, easy install, susceptible to EMI/interference. Hardwired: reliable, secure, higher install cost. Critical industrial systems typically use hardwired for safety."
questions.explanation: Wireless vs. Hardwired is defined as: Wireless: flexible, easy install, susceptible to EMI/interference. Hardwired: reliable, secure, higher install cost. Critical industrial systems typically use hardwired for safety.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "A motor with feedback (encoder) for precise position and speed control. Components: servo motor, encoder, drive/amplifier, controller. Used for CNC, robotics, automation."
questions.explanation: Servo Motor System is defined as: A motor with feedback (encoder) for precise position and speed control. Components: servo motor, encoder, drive/amplifier, controller. Used for CNC, robotics, automation.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Rotates in discrete steps (1.8° typical = 200 steps/rev). Open-loop (no feedback). Precise positioning at low speeds. Used in 3D printers, CNC, and positioning systems."
questions.explanation: Stepper Motor is defined as: Rotates in discrete steps (1.8° typical = 200 steps/rev). Open-loop (no feedback). Precise positioning at low speeds. Used in 3D printers, CNC, and positioning systems.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Temperature: thermocouple, RTD, thermistor. Pressure: strain gauge, Bourdon tube. Proximity: inductive (metal), capacitive (any). Position: encoder, potentiometer, LVDT."
questions.explanation: Sensor Types Summary is defined as: Temperature: thermocouple, RTD, thermistor. Pressure: strain gauge, Bourdon tube. Proximity: inductive (metal), capacitive (any). Position: encoder, potentiometer, LVDT.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Closed-loop: uses feedback to compare actual vs. desired output and self-correct (thermostat). Open-loop: no feedback, output not measured (toaster timer)."

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "P = V x I (Power = Voltage x Current). Also P = I^2 x R, and P = V^2 / R. Measured in Watts."

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Rt = R1 + R2 + R3... Total resistance is the sum of all individual resistors."
questions.explanation: Resistors in Series is defined as: Rt = R1 + R2 + R3... Total resistance is the sum of all individual resistors.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "MA = Length of Effort Arm / Length of Resistance (Load) Arm. Determines the force multiplication of a lever."
questions.explanation: Mechanical Advantage (Lever) is defined as: MA = Length of Effort Arm / Length of Resistance (Load) Arm. Determines the force multiplication of a lever.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Torque (T) = Force (F) x Distance (d). Usually measured in foot-pounds (ft-lb) or Newton-meters (Nm)."

questions.explanation: Resistor Color Band: Blue is defined as: Blue represents the number 6 in standard resistor color coding.

questions.question: Which term is best described by the following: "Pressure control (relief, reducing), Flow control (needle), and Directional control (spool, poppet valves)."

questions.explanation: Engineering Units: Mass is defined as: Measured in slugs (Imperial) or kilograms (Metric). Mass is constant; weight changes with gravity.
